name: Biel through the Puerto de Sierra Mayor and return via the Monasterio de Monlora - circular from Erla
name_original: Biel por el Puerto de Sierra Mayor y vuelta por el Monasterio de Monlora — circular desde Erla
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Erla are characterized by the diverse topography of the Cinco Villas region in Aragon, Spain. The area features varied terrains, from the rolling hills near the Castejón Mountains to more challenging climbs towards the Monasterio de Monlora. Cyclists can navigate low-traffic roads that traverse agricultural landscapes, river valleys like the Arba, and offer panoramic views of the surrounding natural environment. The region's commitment to cycling infrastructure supports a range of routes for different skill levels. Best road cycling routes around Erla The most popular road cycling route is Biel through the Puerto de Sierra Mayor and return via the Monasterio de Monlora - circular from Erla, a 70.3 miles (113.2 km) trail that takes 5 hours 17 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain and views of the Monlora Sanctuary. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is The Romanesque Route of Cinco Villas, a moderate 61.1 miles (98.3 km) path. This route explores the cultural and historical sites within the Cinco Villas region. Local road cyclists also love the Rivas - Farasdues - Asin - Ores via the San Bartolome Reservoir — round trip from Ejea de los Caballeros, a 38.1 miles (61.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain and past the San Bartolome Reservoir, often completed in about 2 hours 50 minutes. Road cycling around Erla is defined by varied topography, low-traffic roads, and views of the Castejón Mountains. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to difficult climbs. The routes in Erla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.5 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 120 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Erla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of road cycling routes can I find around Erla?
A: Erla, located in the Cinco Villas region, offers a diverse range of road cycling routes. You'll find everything from moderate rides through agricultural landscapes and river valleys to more challenging climbs with significant elevation gain, such as the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988484615/biel-through-the-puerto-de-sierra-mayor-and-return-via-the-monasterio-de-monlora-circular-from-erla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Biel through the Puerto de Sierra Mayor and return via the Monasterio de Monlora&lt;/a&gt;. The region's varied topography ensures options for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, experienced riders seeking a challenge will find routes with substantial elevation gain. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988484615/biel-through-the-puerto-de-sierra-mayor-and-return-via-the-monasterio-de-monlora-circular-from-erla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Biel through the Puerto de Sierra Mayor and return via the Monasterio de Monlora&lt;/a&gt; route is classified as difficult, featuring over 1,300 meters of ascent over 113 kilometers. The broader Cinco Villas region also hosts ultra-marathon formats like the 'Desafío 5 Villas' for highly athletic cyclists.

Q: Can I find routes that are suitable for families or less experienced cyclists?
A: While many routes offer moderate challenges, the region's diverse terrain includes options that can be adapted for families or those seeking a gentler ride. Routes often feature low-traffic roads through picturesque agricultural areas. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991273865/rivas-farasdues-asin-ores-via-the-san-bartolome-reservoir-round-trip-from-ejea-de-los-caballeros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rivas - Farasdues - Asin - Ores via the San Bartolome Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate, scenic journey.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Road cycling routes around Erla offer stunning natural and cultural sights. You can enjoy panoramic views of the Cinco Villas region and the distant Moncayo mountain from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3716853" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Monlora and Viewpoint of the Cinco Villas&lt;/a&gt;. Other routes pass by the scenic Arba River or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991273865/rivas-farasdues-asin-ores-via-the-san-bartolome-reservoir-round-trip-from-ejea-de-los-caballeros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Bartolome Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The Castejón Mountains also provide a beautiful backdrop.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes starting from Erla or nearby towns?
A: Yes, many routes in the region are designed as circular tours. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988484615/biel-through-the-puerto-de-sierra-mayor-and-return-via-the-monasterio-de-monlora-circular-from-erla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Biel through the Puerto de Sierra Mayor and return via the Monasterio de Monlora&lt;/a&gt; is a circular route starting from Erla. Another option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993005198/piedratajada-and-marracos-circular-from-valpalmas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piedratajada and Marracos&lt;/a&gt;, a circular route from Valpalmas.

Q: What cultural and historical sites can be explored by road bike?
A: The Cinco Villas region is rich in cultural heritage. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989993946/the-romanesque-route-of-cinco-villas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Romanesque Route of Cinco Villas&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights historical sites. In Ejea de los Caballeros, you can visit historic churches like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5337388" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa María de la Corona&lt;/a&gt;, San Salvador, and Nuestra Señora de la Oliva. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3716853" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Monlora&lt;/a&gt; is another significant religious building accessible by bike.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Erla?
A: The diverse topography of Erla and the Cinco Villas region makes it suitable for cycling across various seasons. However, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for road cycling, avoiding the peak heat of summer while still providing good weather for exploring the varied terrains and scenic views.

Q: Is parking available near the starting points of road cycling routes?
A: Many road cycling routes in the Erla area, particularly those starting from towns like Erla or Ejea de los Caballeros, typically offer parking options. The region is promoted as a cycling hub, and local municipalities often provide facilities for visitors. It's advisable to check specific starting points for detailed parking information.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Erla?
A: The road cycling experience around Erla is highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.5 stars from over 5 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied topography, the low-traffic roads, and the stunning panoramic views, particularly from spots like the Monlora Sanctuary.

Q: Are there any particularly scenic routes with good viewpoints?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its picturesque scenery. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988484615/biel-through-the-puerto-de-sierra-mayor-and-return-via-the-monasterio-de-monlora-circular-from-erla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Biel through the Puerto de Sierra Mayor and return via the Monasterio de Monlora&lt;/a&gt; offers significant elevation gain, rewarding cyclists with impressive views, including those from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3716853" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Monlora and Viewpoint of the Cinco Villas&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5291568" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta de San Jorge&lt;/a&gt; is another notable viewpoint.

Q: How long are the typical road cycling routes in the Erla area?
A: The routes vary significantly in length to accommodate different preferences. You can find moderate routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992739568/ascent-to-alto-de-san-esteban-round-trip-from-ejea-de-los-caballeros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Alto de San Esteban&lt;/a&gt;, which is around 66 kilometers, taking about 2 hours 45 minutes. Longer, more challenging options, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988484615/biel-through-the-puerto-de-sierra-mayor-and-return-via-the-monasterio-de-monlora-circular-from-erla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Biel through the Puerto de Sierra Mayor&lt;/a&gt;, extend to over 113 kilometers and can take more than 5 hours to complete.

Q: What makes Erla a unique destination for road cycling?
A: Erla stands out due to its location within the Cinco Villas region, which actively promotes cycling tourism. The area offers a unique combination of diverse topography, from rolling hills to challenging mountain passes, coupled with low-traffic roads and stunning natural landscapes. This commitment to cycling infrastructure, alongside picturesque scenery and cultural sites, creates a compelling experience for road cyclists.
tips: Known village for being near the spectacular Aguarales of Valpalmas and being the cradle of Santiago Ramón y Cajal where the birth house and museum of the Spanish doctor and scientist and Nobel Prize in Medicine is still preserved

We are located on the outskirts of the town of Biel, in the Altas Cinco Villas region with magnificent views of the town, the church and the imposing castle.
The town of Biel has a very interesting history and the natural environment is beautiful. Without a doubt, it is worth getting lost in its streets.

We are in the Monlora Sanctuary with impressive views of the Cinco Villas region and on clear days we will also see Moncayo.
There is a small parking lot and a restaurant (check dining hours) monlora.com

waypoints.name: View of Biel Castle and Church
Sanctuary of Monlora and Viewpoint of the Cinco Villas
